deviations.description: The process model on page 1 shows that after obtaining patient consent, the nurse performs the 'time out' procedure. Version 2 now requires the doctor to perform the 'time out' procedure. The process violates this stricter requirement because the nurse, not the doctor, conducts the 'time out' procedure.

Version 2 requires that after obtaining patient consent, the next activity must be the 'time out' procedure, performed by the doctor. The process model shows the nurse performing the 'time out' procedure immediately after consent, which does not satisfy the new resource constraint.
process_model_reference: obtain patient consent → time out procedure

deviations.description: The change from 'A_requires_B' (surgical intervention requires consent) to 'A_precedes_B' (consent precedes surgical intervention) does not introduce a stricter requirement, as the process already ensures consent is obtained before surgery.
The explicit assignment of the 'obtain patient consent' activity to the nurse matches the process model, where the nurse is responsible for obtaining consent.
